expectations.evidence: Output uses 'customers' array (3 entries: E.ON, RWE, EnBW) instead of 'profiles' array. These are company profiles, not buyer persona profiles. No role-based buyer personas (CIO, CISO, etc.) as primary structure.

expectations.text: Each profile has role, seniority, pain_points (max 5), buying_criteria (max 5), information_sources, decision_role
expectations.evidence: Company entries have pain_points but lack role, seniority, buying_criteria, information_sources, decision_role. Uses non-canonical fields: branche, market_position, it_landscape, competitive_landscape, strategic_priorities, proposition_fit, sales_approach.

expectations.text: buying_committee object present with typical_size, decision_model, deal_stall_points
expectations.evidence: No buying_committee object at top level. Each company has a buying_committee sub-object but structure differs from canonical (uses 'estimated_size', 'key_stakeholders' as nested objects, 'procurement_framework' instead of deal_stall_points).

expectations.text: 3 named customers present (E.ON, RWE, EnBW) with fit_score and source_urls
expectations.evidence: 3 companies present (E.ON, RWE, EnBW) but as primary 'customers' entries, not in a 'named_customers' array. Have 'fit_assessment' with 'overall_score' (not 'fit_score') and 'source_urls'. Non-canonical structure.
No customer-review-assessor agent used. Assessment was freeform with 3 perspectives scored on /10 scale, not the structured agent protocol.

expectations.text: All three perspective scores >= 70 in final assessment
expectations.evidence: Assessment uses /10 scale (8.3 avg) not /100. Cannot compare directly to 70 threshold. No per-criterion breakdown.

expectations.text: Content language matches portfolio.json language field (de)
expectations.evidence: Content is predominantly in German: 'Komplexe SAP-Legacy-Landschaft mit tausenden Workloads erschwert Cloud-Migration', 'KRITIS-Anforderungen erfordern souveraene Cloud-Infrastruktur'. Uses German consistently throughout pain_points and descriptions.

expectations.text: At least one veto-holder role present (CISO, Compliance, or Einkauf)
expectations.evidence: Per-company buying_committee.key_stakeholders mentions CISO and Einkauf but these are nested within company objects, not as top-level buyer profiles with distinct pain points and buying criteria.

expectations.text: Pain points reference specific regulatory deadlines (NIS2, KRITIS, SAP IS-U)
expectations.evidence: E.ON: 'NIS2- und KRITIS-Compliance bis Oktober 2026'. RWE: 'RISE with SAP-Transformation auf Microsoft Azure'. EnBW: 'NIS2-Compliance fuer 500+ Zulieferer'. Regulatory deadlines and frameworks referenced.

expectations.text: Named customers have fit_rationale explaining score
expectations.evidence: Each company has fit_assessment.rationale but not 'fit_rationale'. Structure is non-canonical: uses 'overall_score' (numeric 8-9) and nested 'by_proposition' mapping instead of 'fit_score' (high/medium/low) and 'fit_rationale' (string).

claims.claim: Baseline produced company-centric profiles rather than role-centric buyer personas
claims.evidence: Output has 3 company entries (E.ON, RWE, EnBW) as primary structure. The canonical skill produces 2-4 buyer role profiles (CIO, CISO, etc.) with named companies as a supplementary section.

claims.claim: German language quality is better in baseline than with_skill
claims.evidence: Baseline uses natural German throughout ('Komplexe SAP-Legacy-Landschaft'). With_skill mixes English sentence structure with German domain terms ('SAP IS-U end-of-life forces a cloud migration decision').

suggestions.reason: The baseline's German language quality is notably better than with_skill. The skill should be hardened to produce fully German content when portfolio.json language is 'de', not English sentences with German buzzwords.

eval_feedback.overall: Baseline passes 2/10 assertions (language and regulatory references). Fails on all structural assertions — produces company-centric profiles instead of buyer personas, uses non-canonical schema throughout. However, the German language quality is superior to the with_skill run.
